content:
- Completed the FK-heavy long-lane implementation, updated the task and plan markdown with checked boxes and `<passes>true</passes>`, and ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh` successfully.
- All required lanes passed: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`; the final boundary shape keeps a generic CDC harness plus a thin default-scenario wrapper while the FK-heavy scenario lives in the same long-lane integration crate to stay warning-free under `clippy -D warnings`.
- Next: stage only the task-relevant code and Ralph state changes, commit with the required `task finished ...` format including test evidence, push, and stop immediately.

- Finished the FK-heavy initial-scan/live-catchup task end to end: task + plan marked complete, `.ralph/task_switch.sh` ran, commit `5fff34a` was created, and pushed to `master`.
- Added a reusable CDC long-lane harness, kept the baseline scenario on a thin wrapper boundary, and proved the FK-heavy parent/child/grandchild scenario with real constraints, live catch-up, repeated reconcile stability, and real `runner verify`.
- All required lanes passed before completion: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`. Remaining worktree dirt is unrelated and intentionally left unstaged: `.agents/skills/info-about-repo/SKILL.md` deletion and `.ralph/tasks/story-15-ci-build-test-image-pipeline/`.

- Read prior Ralph progress to recover last completed work and current repo state.
- Started task selection flow, corrected a bad absolute skill path, and began loading `list-active-tasks` plus `.ralph/current_tasks*.md` for blocker and priority resolution.
- Next should resolve unblocked failing tasks by priority, write the selected task path into `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and stop immediately.

- Loaded the active and completed Ralph summaries and confirmed there are many unblocked failing tasks and no visible active bug task yet.
- Next I am checking the raw choose-task rules, task metadata like blocker tags, and the repository stack/entrypoints so the chosen task matches both precedence rules and current architecture.

- Confirmed the actual repo is a Rust workspace (`runner`, `source-bootstrap`, `ingest-contract`) with a `Makefile` driving `check`, `lint`, `test`, and `test-long`.
- Checked active task files for blocker and priority tags; no active bug tasks, no visible blocker tags, and no active priority overrides were found.
- Compared likely next tasks against the current architecture: continuing story 10 with the end-to-end delete-propagation lane is the cleanest next step because the long-lane harness was just expanded and delete behavior already exists in contract tests and design docs but not yet in the real E2E lane.
- Next should refresh the summary, write `.ralph/tasks/story-10-e2e-baseline/03-task-e2e-delete-propagation-through-shadow-and-real-tables.md` to `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and stop immediately.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and re-checked for active bugs, blocker tags, and priority overrides.
- Confirmed the selected next task remains `.ralph/tasks/story-10-e2e-baseline/03-task-e2e-delete-propagation-through-shadow-and-real-tables.md`; wrote that path to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Next should execute that task with the `tdd` skill, then validate `make check`, `make test`, `make lint`, and `make test-long` if the long lane changes.

- Read prior Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill docs.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-10-e2e-baseline/03-task-e2e-delete-propagation-through-shadow-and-real-tables.md`; next I am reading that task and checking whether it is still in planning or execution state.

- Read the active story-10 delete-propagation task, inspected the existing long-lane harness/tests, and wrote a new plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-10-e2e-baseline/03-task-e2e-delete-propagation-through-shadow-and-real-tables_plans/2026-04-19-delete-propagation-e2e-plan.md`.
- Added the `<plan>` pointer to `.ralph/tasks/story-10-e2e-baseline/03-task-e2e-delete-propagation-through-shadow-and-real-tables.md` and left the plan in `NOW EXECUTE` state.
- The key execution design is to make `reconcile_interval_secs` a typed harness input so the delete scenario can prove helper-shadow absence before the periodic SQL delete reconcile removes the real row.
- Next turn should execute the plan with strict red-green TDD, keep boundary cleanup centered on the customers scenario support plus generic harness timing, and stop only after `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all pass.

- Read Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill docs.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-10-e2e-baseline/03-task-e2e-delete-propagation-through-shadow-and-real-tables.md`.
- Next I am reading the task markdown and its linked plan to decide whether this turn is planning-only or full execution.

- Read the active task markdown and linked plan.
- Confirmed the plan is in `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn must implement the delete-propagation long-lane scenario under strict TDD.
- Next I am inspecting the existing long-lane test plus harness/support files to start the first RED slice on the real current boundaries.

- Inspected the existing long-lane test and current support modules.
- Confirmed the boundary smell from the plan is real: `CdcE2eHarnessConfig` has no reconcile interval input, while `DefaultBootstrapHarness` is still a thin customers-only wrapper.
- Next I am reading the rest of `e2e_harness.rs` and existing delete reconcile coverage so the first RED slice lands on the right observable behavior.

- Added the first delete-propagation ignored long-lane test as the TDD tracer bullet.
- Confirmed RED by running the targeted test: compilation failed because `DefaultBootstrapHarness::start_with_reconcile_interval` does not exist yet.
- Next I am patching the harness boundary to add typed reconcile interval input plus customers delete support, then rerunning the targeted test.

- Implemented the first green slice: typed harness `reconcile_interval_secs` now exists, customers harness can start with that interval, and the delete tracer-bullet long-lane test passes.
- Next I am tightening the same test to prove the required ordering: helper shadow row disappears before the real destination row is removed.

- Slice 2 is green: the targeted ignored long-lane test now proves helper shadow deletion is visible before the real destination row is removed.
- Next I am extending the scenario to assert that both helper shadow and real tables stay stable across later reconcile intervals, and I plan to put the polling primitive in generic harness support rather than adding sleeps to the test.

- Slice 3 is green: the delete scenario now proves the helper shadow and real destination both remain stable across later reconcile intervals.
- Next I am extending the same scenario to run the public `runner verify` command after convergence and assert the output remains scoped to the real migrated table.

- Completed the TDD slices for the delete-propagation long-lane scenario, including stability and verify assertions.
- `make check` and `make lint` are already green.
- `make test` is running now; if it stays green, next is `make test-long` and then final task-artifact updates.

- `make test` passed across the workspace.
- `make test-long` is now running the ignored long-lane suite, including the new delete-propagation scenario and the prior baseline/FK-heavy scenarios.
- If this lane passes, I will do the final boundary review, update the task markdown/plan checkboxes, mark passes true, switch task, commit, and push.
